games.explanation: The market has PIT as a +150 underdog, but our simulation disagrees. Bubba Chandler keeps PIT competitive on the mound against Max Fried. Heat cranks up the offense.

Fried's ERA reads like an ace, but the underlying profile is a lefty who quietly gets tagged harder by righties than lefties, and Pittsburgh happens to be running a righty-heavy lineup at him. Brandon Lowe and Bryan Reynolds have both punished this pitcher archetype all year, and Bubba Chandler brings enough swing-and-miss to keep a Yankees lineup that strikes out plenty from running away with it. The sim has this game inside a tenth of a run, which is not what a +150 dog is supposed to look like against a team the market has decided is a foregone conclusion. Getting a coin flip at plus-money is the entire reason you show up.

The market is treating this like a coin flip because the Athletics wear a big league uniform, but the talent gap between these lineups is not close. Arizona runs out a top four that punishes right-handed pitching from the jump, with Marte and Carroll doing most of the heavy lifting against exactly the arm profile they'll see tonight. Merrill Kelly isn't overpowering, but he doesn't need to be against a group that leans on Nick Kurtz to carry the water, and a closed roof in a neutral run environment favors the side with the deeper card. Getting the better team at a shade under even money is the entire ballgame.

picks.explanation: Fried's ERA reads like an ace, but the underlying profile is a lefty who quietly gets tagged harder by righties than lefties, and Pittsburgh happens to be running a righty-heavy lineup at him. The market is treating this like a coin flip because the Athletics wear a big league uniform, but the talent gap between these lineups is not close. Arizona runs out a top four that punishes right-handed pitching from the jump, with Marte and Carroll doing most of the heavy lifting against exactly the arm profile they'll see tonight. Getting the better team at a shade under even money is the entire ballgame.

Seattle is being priced like the clearly better team, which ignores that they're handing the ball to a starter who has spent all year giving up loud contact to anyone breathing. Emerson Hancock has walked more than ten percent of the lefties he's faced and let them post a .391 xwOBA against him, which is a problem when the Reds can roll out a left-handed heavy top of the order in a park that plays fair. Singer is the steadier arm here, and the projected margin is well inside a run. Getting a run and a half at this number on a game the sims see as a coin flip is the entire ballgame.
